# Phase B Decision Tree — D7+ playbook

**Triggered by:** `D6_FINAL_VERDICT.md` (auto-generated by d6_autotrigger.timer ≈ Apr 30 21:00 UTC)
**Status:** Pre-written at 86.7h dry-run mark · 2026-04-28
**Purpose:** When the Telegram alert fires, this doc tells you exactly what to do — no fresh decisions required.

---

## How to read this doc

1. Telegram delivers `🟢 D6 BINDING VERDICT — GO` **or** `🛑 D6 BINDING VERDICT — NO-GO`
2. Open `D6_FINAL_VERDICT.md` for the named lead cell + maker-net bps
3. Match the verdict to the branch below
4. Execute the listed actions, in order

D7 begins when ADR-003 expires (May 1). Until then: NO new strategy code on main.

---

## BRANCH A — 🟢 GO  (≥1 cell with maker-net ≥ +5 bps + stable across 4/5 days + monotonicity ≥ 0.85)

**Read first:** the verdict's "Lead cell" line. That's the candidate. Everything below is execution.

### A.1 — Pre-D7 (last hours of Apr 30)
- [ ] Read `D6_FINAL_VERDICT.md` end-to-end. Note lead cell `(pair, feature, horizon)` + maker-net bps.
- [ ] Skim `D6_FINAL_NATURE.txt` for the full per-quintile table on the lead cell. Verify the Q5−Q1 economic interpretation makes sense (e.g. high spread → mean reversion, high funding → unwind).
- [ ] If verdict shows ≥3 independent cells, read the "Robust hits ≥3 pairs" section — that's the ensemble option.

### A.2 — D7 morning (May 1, ADR-003 expires)
- [ ] Create feature branch: `feat/phase-b-{leadfeature}-{horizon}` (e.g. `feat/phase-b-funding_rate_8h-1800s`)
- [ ] Implement strategy in `gods_level_engine.py` as a new `class PhaseB{LeadFeature}Strategy` following the existing `class Consensus` pattern:
  - `init()` reads the feature + horizon from config
  - `next()` enters when feature crosses Q5 (or Q1 for negative-IC cells) AND Q1 forward bias is +
  - Exit on horizon expiry OR HARD_STOP_PCT (Patch 1)
- [ ] Add maker-execution path (post-only limit orders) as a separate branch — verdict guides whether maker is required
- [ ] Add config flag in `run.py`: `--strategy phase-b-{name}`
- [ ] Tests in `tests/test_phase_b.py`:
  - Entry: feature in Q5 → BUY signal
  - Exit at horizon: position closes
  - Exit at hard stop: position closes
  - No spurious entries during Q3
- [ ] Push branch, run pytest

### A.3 — D7 afternoon — walk-forward validation
- [ ] Run `python rsi2_validate.py`-equivalent on the new strategy (4-gate test):
  - Rolling walk-forward (5 windows)
  - Monte Carlo permutation
  - Fee stress @ 0.20% / 0.30% / 0.40%
  - Bootstrap CI on Q5−Q1 magnitude
- [ ] Gate: ≥3 of 4 must pass. If any 2 fail → STOP, do NOT deploy. Return to research.

### A.4 — D7+1 to D7+3 — deploy on XRP_USDT (single-pair focus)
- [ ] Stop `trading-bot@XRP_USDT.service`
- [ ] Update systemd unit: `--strategy phase-b-{name} --interval {horizon-appropriate}`
- [ ] Restart
- [ ] Verify first trade fires within expected window (per Q5−Q1 frequency)
- [ ] Keep ETH/SOL/AVAX on Vote ensemble as decorrelated control

### A.5 — D7+3 to Stage 1 gate (60 days, Jul ~7)
- [ ] `production-monitor.timer` already alerts on DD/restart/inactive. No additional ops work.
- [ ] Weekly review: trade count, WR, avg R, Sharpe vs buy-and-hold, MDD.
- [ ] Stage 1 promotion gate (per `CHAMPION_MODE.md`):
  - ≥30 closed trades
  - Cumulative paper PnL > 0
  - DD < 5%
  - Sharpe ≥ 0.5
  - Live trade rate within 30% of D6 expected frequency
- [ ] If gate cleared → BRANCH C (Stage 2)
- [ ] If failed → BRANCH D (research return)

### Risk overlays (mandatory, all branches)
- HARD_STOP_PCT = 0.025 (Patch 1)
- max_drawdown_pct = 5.0 (Patch 3)
- bar-close gate (Patch 5)
- Position size: 0.5% of equity per entry (NOT 1% — Phase B is unproven)
- Max concurrent positions: 1 (single-pair focus)

---

## BRANCH B — 🛑 NO-GO  (zero cells clear maker-fee gate, OR no cells survive 7 filters)

The 86.7h dry-run already trended this direction (47 → 40 → 20 survivors as data accumulated, 1 → 1 → 0 maker-fee winners). NO-GO is the most likely outcome.

This is **not a project failure.** It's the discipline framework saving capital. Microstructure-single-feature on Gate.io spot doesn't work as a directional Phase B strategy. We learn and pivot.

### B.1 — Same day verdict arrives
- [ ] Read full `D6_FINAL_VERDICT.md` and audit-trail logs to confirm NO-GO is real, not a tooling bug
- [ ] Verify the survivor list still has cells with high IC but low magnitude (signal real, just too small)
- [ ] Update `CLAUDE.md` and `ADR-002-phase-b-direction.md`: microstructure-single-feature path closed

### B.2 — D7+1 to D7+3 — choose B-prime
Three credible pivots, each with prebuilt research scaffolding ready:

#### B.2.1 — Multi-feature ensemble (compound small signals)
**Premise:** 4 independent +3 bps signals firing simultaneously = +12 bps net edge

**Work:**
- [ ] Identify the surviving feature clusters from D6_FINAL_ROBUST.txt (after de-duplication via filter 6)
- [ ] Build `phase_b_ensemble.py` (NEW research code, feature branch) that:
  - Computes alignment score: sum of normalised signed IC contributions
  - Fires only when ≥3 of 4 features agree on direction
  - Backtest on the same 5-day data
- [ ] Validation gate: backtested net edge after maker fees ≥ +5 bps over 60+ days of synthetic walk-forward
- [ ] If passes → A.4 deployment path
- [ ] If fails → B.2.2 or B.2.3

**Pros:** Reuses everything we built. Same data. Direct extension.
**Cons:** Ensembles tend to overfit; small sample size for alignment events.

#### B.2.2 — Maker-execution simulation harness
**Premise:** Even at 14 bps gross edge (XRP spread@1800s @ 16h), maker fees of 10 bps RT leave +4 bps net. With realistic fill-rate modelling, maybe profitable.

**Work:**
- [ ] Build `maker_simulator.py`: replays parquet data, simulates passive limit orders at top-of-book, tracks fill rate vs mid drift
- [ ] Re-run D6 surviving cells through the simulator
- [ ] If any cell shows ≥+5 bps net edge AND ≥30% fill rate → A.4 deployment path

**Pros:** Lower fee tier unlocks the magnitude problem.
**Cons:** Fill-rate modelling is hard; passive quotes get adversely selected.

#### B.2.3 — ADR-002 fallback B1: on-chain signals
**Premise:** Microstructure thesis exhausted; pivot to on-chain data (whale flows, exchange netflows, miner transfers, stablecoin issuance).

**Work:**
- [ ] Add data sources: Glassnode-equivalent (on-chain flow API) or directly parse mempool data
- [ ] Build `onchain_collector.py` mirroring `microstructure_collector.py` schema
- [ ] Restart 5-day data accumulation window
- [ ] Run same 7-filter battery on the new feature set
- [ ] If any feature clears: A.1+ deployment path

**Pros:** Different signal class, decorrelated from microstructure. Industry-validated edge sources.
**Cons:** New 5-day collection cycle. May find the same magnitude-vs-fee problem.

### B.3 — Decision: which B-prime?
Score each option:

| | Effort | Probability of edge | Time-to-validate |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| B.2.1 ensemble | low | medium | 1 week |
| B.2.2 maker | medium | medium | 1-2 weeks |
| B.2.3 on-chain | high | high | 5+ weeks |

**Default order:** B.2.1 first (cheapest, fastest, reuses data). If fails, B.2.2. If both fail, B.2.3 with full 5-day collection cycle.

### B.4 — During B-prime work
- [ ] Vote ensemble continues running on all 4 pairs (paper)
- [ ] `production-monitor.timer` continues alerting
- [ ] No live capital deployed
- [ ] ADR-003 lessons hold: NEW strategy code goes through 4-gate validation before reaching main

---

## BRANCH C — Stage 1 paper gate cleared (≈ Jul 7, 60 days post-D7)

**Trigger:** ≥30 closed trades, cumulative PnL > 0, DD < 5%, Sharpe ≥ 0.5, live trade rate within 30% of expected.

### C.1 — Real-money preflight
- [ ] Verify `LIVE_MODE = True` toggle exists in `run.py` and is documented
- [ ] Verify Gate.io API keys configured for the paper-real bot's pair (XRP_USDT)
- [ ] Verify `MAX_POSITION_USDT` cap = $200 (not $1000 default)
- [ ] Verify daily Telegram PnL summary cron runs (build it if missing)
- [ ] Verify emergency-stop circuit: 10% intraday DD or 3 consecutive losses → service kills itself

### C.2 — Stage 2 deployment (1 day)
- [ ] Stop `trading-bot@XRP_USDT.service`
- [ ] Update unit: `--live-mode --max-usdt 200 --emergency-stop-pct 10`
- [ ] Restart
- [ ] First Telegram alert when first real-money order fills
- [ ] Daily 00:00 UTC PnL summary

### C.3 — Stage 2 validation window (60-90 days)
- [ ] Track daily PnL via Telegram
- [ ] Weekly Sharpe + DD review
- [ ] Stage 2 promotion gate (per `CHAMPION_MODE.md`):
  - 60+ days continuous live operation
  - Net real PnL > $0
  - No emergency stops fired
  - At least one regime change survived (bull → bear or vice versa)
- [ ] If passes → BRANCH E (scale per CHAMPION_MODE)
- [ ] If fails → BRANCH D

---

## BRANCH D — Stage gate failed (any stage)

**Trigger:** any Stage 1 or Stage 2 metric below threshold.

### D.1 — Triage (same day)
- [ ] Stop the failing bot
- [ ] Pull full trade log into a notebook
- [ ] Compute: WR, avg R, MDD, Sharpe, vs B&H, vs backtest expectation
- [ ] Identify single-cause vs systemic failure

### D.2 — Diagnosis branches
- **Single regime mismatch** (bot designed for trend, market chopped): keep on shelf, redeploy when regime returns
- **Backtest-vs-live divergence** (e.g. slippage > expected): re-run with realistic execution model
- **Edge decayed** (signal worked at D6, dead at deployment): revisit feature engineering, accept that microstructure edges decay quickly
- **Bug in code** (rare given test coverage): fix, re-validate via 4-gate

### D.3 — Decision
- [ ] If fixable → return to BRANCH A.3 (re-validate)
- [ ] If unfixable → return to BRANCH B (next B-prime)

---

## BRANCH E — Stage 2 cleared, scale

Per `CHAMPION_MODE.md` doctrine. Each tier 60+ days minimum:

| Tier | Cap | Gate |
|---|---:|---|
| Stage 2 | $200 | net PnL > 0 over 60d real |
| Stage 3 | $1,000 | net PnL > 0 over 60d at $200 |
| Stage 4 | $10,000 | net PnL > 0 over 60d at $1k |
| Stage 5 | $100,000 | net PnL > 0 over 60d at $10k |
| L99 | $1M+ | sustained edge across regime changes |

**This is the L99/godslevel claim — earned via track record, not declared.**

---

## What this doc replaces

- "What do we do when D6 fires?" — answered, by branch
- "What does NO-GO mean for the project?" — not failure, just pivot to B-prime
- "How do we go from green verdict to live capital?" — A.1 → A.5 → C → E
- "What's the minimum bar for each promotion?" — explicit gates per branch

When D6 fires, read the matching branch top-to-bottom and execute. No fresh thinking required.

---

## ADR linkage

- **ADR-001** — D-day discipline: validation BEFORE deployment
- **ADR-002** — Phase B direction: this doc operationalizes it
- **ADR-003** — D7 freeze: expires May 1, this doc is what May 1+ work looks like
- **GODMODE_AUDIT.md** — composite 27/100, 5 patches, fee-aware gates
- **CHAMPION_MODE.md** — staged capital scaling doctrine

When in doubt, the rule is: **no live capital before validated edge. No new strategy code on main without 4-gate test pass.**
